briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady pre-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for pre-spawn with clear water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In Florida heavy grass, switch to 30-lb braid with a 12-inch monofilament leader (Sufix Elite) for topwater — need to force fish out of hay-field grass or you'll lose them
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'So important in clear water lakes that you've got good polarized glasses.' They let you see green grass clumps further out so you can pitch to them without getting right on top of spooky fish.

Wheeler: Prefers monofilament over braid for topwater because braid's zero stretch risks jerking the bait out of the fish's mouth when anglers instinctively jerk on the blowup. Mono's stretch provides forgiveness. Only exception is Florida heavy g...

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable pre-spawn conditions mean fish are staging predictably at 5-12ft on points, channel swings, and riprap banks. They're feeding aggressively as water warms. Work the transition zone between deep winter haunts and shallow spawning areas.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
